repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 says ExactID/PartialID catalogues are seed-time empty

published_prior_art.cite: DoudchenkoImbens2016 allows negative weights and relaxed SC constraints, but does not state the sharp bounded-error l1 multiplier

repo_prior_art.cite: none; q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends concerns aggregate DID pretrend summaries, not fixed SC weights or oracle bridge intervals

published_prior_art.cite: NazaretShiBlei2024 and FermanPinto2021 give misspecification/imperfect-fit bounds, but not this fixed-weight sharp support-function interval

repo_prior_art.cite: none; archived same-qid angle described row-span visibility and latent-dictionary unions rather than affine-nullspace l1 radius separation

published_prior_art.cite: KelloggMogstadPouliotTorgovitsky2021 studies interpolation/extrapolation bias tradeoffs via MASC, not equal-prefit sharp partial-ID radius separation

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue scalar-prefit sensitivity impossibility for SC partial-ID intervals

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoPossebom2018, ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021, and CattaneoFengTitiunik2021 are adjacent uncertainty/sensitivity comparators, but do not subsume the deterministic scalar-rule obstruction

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses CattaneoFengTitiunik2021, 'Prediction Intervals for Synthetic Control Methods,' a closer JASA uncertainty-quantification comparator for SC intervals and sensitivity than several cited inference papers; it appears orthogonal rather than subsuming, but must be discussed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Cattaneo, Feng, and Titiunik (2021, JASA) to §5 and to the Theorem 2 or Conjecture 2 novelty block, explicitly separating probabilistic prediction intervals from deterministic sharp partial-ID intervals.
Promote Conjecture 2 to a concrete two-design theorem using Theorem 2: hold (q, eta, c) fixed while varying either l1 weight leverage or bridge l1 leverage, then compute the two radii.

State the Conjecture 1 open-class condition as an explicit rank/interior-margin condition, including how delta is obtained from the distance of an interior convex solution to the simplex boundary along a null direction.
Add a boundary corollary comparing convex weights (radius 2 eta times bridge leverage) against signed affine weights with the same pre-fit, so the applied reporting implication is immediate.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around SC uncertainty quantification, especially CattaneoFengTitiunik2021, then keep the kernel centered on Conjecture 1.
